qualificationOriginal: Diploma di Superamento dell'Esame di Stato conclusivo dei Corsi di Istruzione Secondaria Superiore
accessRequirements: Grants access to higher education in Italy and internationally, subject to specific university requirements.
additionalNotes: Must be obtained after a minimum of 12 years of global schooling for most international recognitions.

awardingBody: State Boards of Education / Local School Districts
accessRequirements: For Italian universities, a US High School Diploma must typically be accompanied by 3 qualifying AP exams or 1 year of university-level study to meet the 12-year requirement with academic rigor.
additionalNotes: The SAT/ACT may also be required depending on the institution.

accessRequirements: Generally accepted for undergraduate admission globally. For Italy, it meets the minimum 12-year schooling requirement.
additionalNotes: Specific subject requirements depend on the chosen university program.

qualificationOriginal: General Certificate of Education Advanced Level (A-Levels)
awardingBody: Various Examination Boards (e.g., Cambridge, Edexcel, AQA)
accessRequirements: Requires a minimum of 3 A-Level passes in relevant subjects. For Italy, passing 3 A-levels is strictly required.
additionalNotes: O-Levels / GCSEs alone are not sufficient for university entry.

awardingBody: Ministries of Education of the Federal States
accessRequirements: Grants direct access to all subjects at all higher education institutions in Germany and across Europe.
additionalNotes: Subject-specific Fachgebundene Hochschulreife only grants access to certain subjects.

accessRequirements: Grants direct access to higher education in France. Recognized globally for university admission.
additionalNotes: Certain selective programs (Classes Préparatoires) require specific Baccalauréat specializations and high grades.
